title: Observation of Non-Markovian Evolution of Tripartite Quantum Steering
authors: Yan Wang, Shao-qi Lin, Rui-qi Shen, Fang-liang Chen, Fang-liang Chen, Fang-liang Chen, Yong-nan Sun, Qi-ping Su, Chui-ping Yang
abstract: The memory effects in open quantum systems can induce information backflow and revive quantum correlations, thereby providing a powerful way to protect and recover useful quantum resources in realistic noisy environments. However, such dynamics remains experimentally unexplored in multipartite quantum steering. Here we observe different non-Markovian evolution of tripartite quantum steering using Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger-type mixed states, covering both death and revival processes. In particular, we experimentally demonstrate the more intricate asymmetric steering structure of tripartite quantum steering through different bipartitions, which do not arise in bipartite systems. Our results provide foundational insights into the hierarchical and directional structures in multipartite quantum steering, and highlight its potential as a useful resource for asymmetric quantum information processing.
summary: This paper reports the experimental observation of non-Markovian evolution in tripartite quantum steering using photon polarization and path modes. The researchers demonstrated that quantum correlations can undergo 'sudden death' and subsequent 'revival' due to environmental memory effects. They also uncovered an asymmetric steering structure across different bipartitions, a feature absent in simpler bipartite systems. These findings highlight the potential of using non-Markovian environments to protect and recover multipartite quantum resources for asymmetric quantum information processing.
main_problem: The study investigates the non-Markovian evolution of tripartite quantum steering, specifically looking for the death and revival of quantum correlations and the asymmetric, hierarchical structures of steering in multipartite systems under decoherence.
main_result: The researchers experimentally observed the 'death and revival' of tripartite quantum steering and demonstrated a complex asymmetric steering structure across different bipartitions that does not exist in bipartite systems.
method: The team used an optical setup with polarization-entangled photon pairs, employing an unbalanced interferometer and quartz plates to engineer time-dependent dephasing and memory effects, followed by three-qubit quantum state tomography.
model_or_system: The physical system consists of tripartite GHZ-type mixed states encoded in the path and polarization degrees of freedom of photons, interacting with an environment via a dephasing mechanism.
key_observables: One-sided device-independent (1SDI) and two-sided device-independent (2SDI) steering, entanglement witness (EW), and the degree of non-Markovianity (M).
important_parameters: Purity of the GHZ state (eta), control parameter for the superposition state (theta), and the degree of non-Markovianity (M).
main_assumptions: The initial global state is assumed to be factorized, and the model assumes Bob and Charlie share the same environmental frequency.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a theoretical illustration of the tripartite non-Markovian evolution and transitions between steering regimes; Figure 2 details the experimental setup including the PPKTP crystal, interferometer, and measurement apparatus.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of multipartite steering in non-Markovian environments, describes the experimental setup and state preparation, presents the results of the steering death and revival processes, analyzes the asymmetric hierarchical structures, and concludes with implications for quantum information processing.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for researchers in open quantum systems and quantum optics as it provides experimental evidence of how memory effects can revive multipartite quantum resources and reveals new directional dynamics unique to multipartite systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Theoretical illustration of ideal tripartite non- Markovian evolution. The subsystem (A) of tripartite state (composed of A, B and C) undergoes the time-dependent interactions (varying with t) with its surrounding environ- ments (E) denoted with operations U1 and U2. The whole tripartite quantum steering dynamical evolution, including the sudden death and revival processes, covers steerable in one-sided and two-sided device-independent (1SDI and 2SDI) scenarios, only steerable in 2SDI scenario, and unsteerable in both scenarios.

FIG. 2. Experimental setup. Polarization-entangled pho- ton pairs are generated via parametric down-conversion in a periodically poled potassium titanyl phosphate (PPKTP) crystal. One photon is sent to the interference which sepa- rated and combined by two beam displacers (BDs), while the other one passes through the unbalanced interferometer (UI) in the blue dashed box, preparing the initial system state ρABC(θ, η). On Alice’s side, the sequence of quartz plates (QPs) at 0◦and 90◦are respectively inserted to introduce the time-dependent operations U1 and U2 corresponding to the orange and green boxes. We mark the QPs at 90◦in the green box, leaving the rest at 0◦in the blue and orange...

FIG. 3. The non-Markovian evolution processes of ini- tial system state ρABC(θ = π/4, η = 1). (a) The evolution of entanglement witness (EW) versus the total thickness of quartz plates L(λ), with the entangled region in light blue re- gion. (b) The evolution of steering witness (SW) in one-sided and two-sided device-independent scenarios (1SDI and 2SDI) versus L(λ), with experimental results in hollow triangles and circles. The dark, light, and unshaded backgrounds are de- noted the states steerable in 1SDI and 2SDI scenarios, steer- able only in 2SDI scenarios, and unsteerable in both regions. The red dotted line denotes the entanglement boundary of L(λ). All error bars are calculated...

FIG. 4. Different configurations of non-Markovian evolution processes of system state (ρABC(θ = π/4, η = 0.66)). (a) Alice versus Bob and Charlie (A|BC). (b) Bob versus Alice and Charlie (B|AC). (c) Charlie versus Alice and Bob (C|AB). The horizontal axis is the total thickness of quartz plates L(λ), and the vertical axis is the degree of tri- partite steering witness (SW), with the steerable boundaries in gray dashed lines. Hollow triangles and circles represent SW in one-sided and two-sided device independent scenar- ios (1SDI and 2SDI). The dark, light, and unshaded back- grounds are denoted states steerable in 1SDI and 2SDI sce- narios, steerable only in 2SDI scenarios, and unsteerable...
